entries.text: You open your eyes. Straw beneath you, the smell of wet rice, a low roof you do not know. A cold spring — the ninth year of An'ei — though the year is as lost to you as your name.

"You're awake." Sōan the physician sits back on his heels. "No kami carried you off, whatever the village wants to believe. A flood took you, and a blow to the head took the rest. Bodies forget. Given work and rice, they also mend."
entries.speaker: Sōan

entries.text: You take stock of yourself: bruised, hollow, half-starved — but breathing.
Rice lies scattered across the Kurosawa grain-store floor. Spilled stores are waste — and clearing waste is a kind of work, if you set your hands to it.
"Then I'll trust your craft, not the village's ghosts."
entries.speaker: You

entries.text: "Sense, at last. Rest, eat, and let the swelling go down. The wits come back last — don't force them."
entries.speaker: Sōan

entries.text: Perk unlocked — Sōan's Counsel: A mind honed sharper than the body it wears. (+1 INT / −1 STR)
Something surfaces and is gone — a porter's knot, a road in grey rain, a name you cannot keep hold of.
"Hold the road. The rain. Almost a name."
entries.speaker: You

entries.text: You chase it inward — and the ache in your skull chases you back. The name stays lost, but the habit of looking sets in.
Perk unlocked — The Inward Turn: A mind that deepens by dwelling, at the price of a slower body. (+1 INT / −1 SPD)

On your feet, then. I am Genemon, steward of this house, and I keep the little it has left to keep. You'll not remember any of it, so hear it plain: the Kurosawa are samurai still — on the lord's rolls, if nowhere in the granary.
entries.speaker: Genemon

entries.text: "I'll earn my keep. Point me at it."
entries.speaker: You

entries.text: "...Good. The house has had its fill of hands that don't. We'll see if you mean it."
entries.speaker: Genemon

entries.text: Perk unlocked — Genemon's Charge: Honest muscle set plainly to the task — sure over nimble. (+1 STR / −1 AGI)

So you put your hands to it. Rake what grain is still whole back toward the basket, a fistful at a time. We reckon a samurai house’s worth in koku — a year’s eating for one man — and every basket you save us is a measure we need not go begging to the lord to make good.
entries.speaker: Genemon

entries.text: Your arms are leaden. You could rest a moment against the cool post.
Clear it without my standing over you, and you'll have earned your rice and a dry corner to sleep in. I'll make you no grander promise than that. In this house, none of us holds one.
entries.speaker: Genemon

entries.text: "...You don't shirk the work," Genemon says, eyeing the cleared boards and the filling basket. "The house has had its fill of hands that do. Earn your keep, and a place here is yours."
Dawn at the gate. The forecourt is swept clean — your doing — and Genemon waits by the posts, watching you the way a man watches weather.
"You cleared the stores without being told twice. The house is short of hands and shorter of trustworthy ones. Stay — you're no day-hire now. Earn your rice."
entries.speaker: Genemon

entries.text: "The gate and the swept forecourt are yours to work now; stores come and go here. The home paddies too — the rice that feeds the house. And the kura's own repair is yours to tend: spend the house's small surplus to shore it up."
entries.speaker: Genemon

entries.text: A pack-laden stranger has laid a mat in the lee of the gate-post — an Ōmi pedlar, come because a tended gate draws trade. He lifts a hand as you pass.
"A tended gate's a lucky gate for a man with a pack. Tokubei, of Ōmi — mind if I keep my mat here a while, young master? Coin of your own spends as well as any lord's."
entries.speaker: Tokubei

entries.text: "The house has my hands."
entries.speaker: You

entries.text: "...Good. Hands that don't need watching are the rarest thing I keep."
entries.speaker: Genemon

entries.text: The gate and the swept forecourt are yours to work now — stores come and go here.
The terraced home paddies open to you — the rice that feeds the house.
You begin to mark the turning of the days, and the four seasons with them.
Rank ↑ — Kept hand 下人
The estate's own state of repair is yours to tend now — spend the house's surplus to shore it up.
The rice you clear and grow is food, not only trade — a plain bowl of it fills a hollow belly and puts you back to work the faster.
The near hill in first light. Genemon leads you past the forecourt for the first time, out to where the woodlot meets the wild edge of the satoyama.

"You can be set a task and trusted to finish it out of my sight — worth more than a strong back. The woodlot and the near hill are yours to work now; the house needs fuel and forage, and it trusts you to bring them in."
entries.speaker: Genemon

entries.text: "One more thing, and not a small one. A wolf's been at the grain stores in the night. Someone must face it — and there is no one else to send. Think on it."
entries.speaker: Genemon

entries.text: A lean man about your own age ambles up from the wood-stack, an axe on one shoulder, grinning as if you two already share a joke.
"Rokusuke — kept on two winters back, so I know where the bodies are buried. Do the work, keep your head down, don't let the old steward catch you idle. That's the whole of it."
entries.speaker: Rokusuke

entries.text: Knotting a load for the woodlot, your fingers tie a porter's knot you never learned — quick, certain, a stranger's habit in your own hands. It means nothing. It will not leave you.
"The stores are the house's life. I'll face it."
entries.speaker: You

entries.text: "...Huh. Most men find a reason to be elsewhere. You might do."
entries.speaker: Rokusuke

entries.text: A way to track what your hands are learning takes shape — your Skills.
The stable yard and woodlot edge — fuel, timber, and the first sight of the road out.
Above the estate lies the near satoyama — sansai to gather, and the first edge of danger.
Beyond the near hill the ground climbs into the deep satoyama — a richer forage for those who dare it, and the boar in its wallow.
A wolf has been at the grain stores in the night, bold with hunger. Someone must face it — and there is no one else to send.
Rank ↑ — Trusted hand 手代
You could boil the wild greens into a hot meal — plain fare, but a warm meal is what closes wounds and mends a body after a fight.
The wolf comes out of the dark among the rice-sacks. You swing the pole, miss, swing again — and somehow, more luck than skill, it bolts bleeding into the night. You are alive. You should not be.

Kihei the drillmaster finds you shaking by the stores. He says nothing for a long moment. Then: "You lived. That's the only talent that matters in the end. Come to the yard at dawn — I'll teach you the rest."

The drill yard behind the omoya, first light. You've stood over the grain-store wolf's carcass; word travels. A hard-faced man is already there, a bokken in each hand — and he throws you one without a word of greeting.
"So. You put down the thing that had the run of our stores. Farmers don't do that. There's a soldier in you under the farmhand — I've watched it a week and I'm done pretending I haven't."
entries.speaker: Kihei

entries.text: "You're gate-watch now: a weapon, a yard to train in, and the estate's defence on your shoulders — pests, beasts, and the masterless men who drift down the woodlot road. Keep a field-guide of what you face; a soldier who knows his enemy outlives one who doesn't."
entries.speaker: Kihei

entries.text: "Show me how to end a fight fast."
entries.speaker: You

entries.text: "Fast, he says. Fast gets you a spear in the back. But there's fire in it — we'll aim it before it burns you."
entries.speaker: Kihei

entries.text: The drill yard opens to you — a place to train, and foes that must be met.
You begin to keep a bestiary — a field-guide of the beasts of the estate, filling in as you face each one.

Standing in the drill yard, you begin to grasp how a house like this is truly weighed — not by its rice alone, but by its arms, its lands and coin, its standing before the lord, and the worth of its name. That reckoning sits far above a gate-watch. For now, you only see that it is there.
Rank ↑ — Gate-watch 門番
A dry corner of the grain-store is kept back for you now — a straw mat, a bowl, a place to lay your head. "A place here is yours," the old man said. Small, but yours to keep.
The crop-raiding monkey overcomes you; you limp home badly used. (HP 1→1) You drop 2578 rice in the rout. Eat and mend before you take the field again.
Work the home paddies. (+5 rice)
You lie down in your corner, on the thin straw mat. The ache dulls; the light through the slats shifts.
Work the home paddies. (+6 rice)
Work the home paddies. (+5 rice)

Work the home paddies. (+5 rice)
You lie down in your corner, on the thin straw mat. The ache dulls; the light through the slats shifts.
Work the home paddies. (+6 rice)
Work the home paddies. (+5 rice)
Genemon meets you at the kura door with an iron key on a cord, worn smooth by other hands before yours.
"The kura key. Mind the stores as if the rice were your own — from today it half is. The house is forgetting you were ever a stranger. So am I."
entries.speaker: Genemon

entries.text: He walks you on to the woodlot smithy, where a bent old man coaxes an edge back onto a mattock. He doesn't look up.

"Tōzō. I keep the estate's iron. A blade you don't tend turns on you — bring me the hides and the metal off what you kill, and I'll show you what an edge wants. The forge is yours to use now; try not to ruin my fire."
entries.speaker: Tōzō

entries.text: "And the omoya's shuttered rooms are aired and swept — the house rises, and you'll walk floors the family walks. Don't let it turn your head."
entries.speaker: Genemon

entries.text: "Every grain accounted."
entries.speaker: You

entries.text: "Spoken like a steward. Good — the house was bled white once by hands that weren't."
entries.speaker: Genemon

entries.text: You learn to read the wear on an edge — a blade dulls and chips with use, and a dull edge bites less. Mind your weapon's condition now.
The woodlot smithy is yours to use — strip what the carcasses give up, forge a real edge, keep it repaired, and take up whichever weapon suits the watch.
The omoya — the main house — is opened to you. Its shuttered rooms, closed since the lean years, are aired and swept; you walk floors the family walks.
Rank ↑ — Kura-warden 蔵番
The crop-raiding monkey overcomes you; you limp home badly used. (HP 1→1) You drop 3591 rice in the rout. Eat and mend before you take the field again.
